u-eris.name: ErisWanderer
u-eris.tagline: hunts the strange, archives the stranger
u-eris.signature: [i]"If the map disagrees with the ground, trust the ground."[/i]

u-freeman.name: CaptainFreeman
u-freeman.tagline: still convinced the basement door moves
u-freeman.signature: [b]Forum rule 3:[/b] no posting co-ords past midnight.

p-1.body:
Alright, back from the ridge. Putting this up before I lose signal again.

[b]Summary:[/b] the trail marker at grid [color=#b45309]K-17[/color] is [s]missing[/s] [b]moved[/b]. Fresh cut, no moss. Someone rearranged it in the last [size=4]two weeks[/size].

I'm not saying anything yet. Just logging it.

p-2.body:
Thanks for the log. Attaching this to the active watch thread.

Reminder to everyone: [mark]do not go up there alone.[/mark] Pair up, leave a chit with the lodge, check in by 1800.

[i]— locking replies overnight, reopen tomorrow.[/i]

p-3.body:
First post here, sorry if this is the wrong place.

I was on the ridge two weekends ago and I thought I was seeing things, but the stone at K-17 was already pointing [b]east[/b]. The ranger at the lodge told me it was supposed to point north.

I have a photo if it helps. Can DM.

p-4.body:
Welcome newcomers. Quick housekeeping before you dive in.

[b]1.[/b] Read the pinned [i]Trail Etiquette[/i] post before asking about access.
[b]2.[/b] Use [color=#b45309]grid coordinates[/color], not landmarks — landmarks move (see: K-17).
[b]3.[/b] No photos of other members without consent.

That's it. Be excellent to each other.

p-5.body: Adding to this: if you're new and you see something [mark]weird[/mark] on the ridge, don't post a vague "I saw something" — write down the grid, the time, and what direction you were facing. Details or it didn't happen.

t-ridge.title: Ridge marker K-17 — moved, or am I losing it?
